summary: Cycle B (2nd cron fire of UTC 2026-05-24; cycle A closed 2026-05-24T05:07:45Z, cycle B opens 06:00:56Z = ~53 min wall-clock gap — RETURNS to ~1h cadence and BREAKS the 'ESTABLISHED with 3 observations' claim from cycle A close). Ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 36th consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED at 06:01:40Z, round-trip 0 failures sustained (24s elapsed; heartbeat written 06:01:59Z status=PASS, 4-field schema with no `source` field — confirms regen-all write path per dual-write-path model). FIVE substantive findings beyond pure heartbeat: (1) CADENCE CLAIM FALSIFIED. Cycle A's 'ESTABLISHED ~5h cadence' claim is broken by cycle B's ~53 min gap. Cadence is bimodal/multimodal (~1h G→K + A→B; ~5h E→F + K→L + L→M + M→A). Carry-over #1 DOWNGRADED from cycle A's 'ESTABLISHED' back to 'bimodal/multimodal; cycle A overcalibrated, cycle B corrects'. Critic metric-integrity SELF-CORRECTION flag captured — cycle A's 3-observation claim was insufficient sample; should have demanded 4-6 obs before declaring established. (2) TOKEN THRESHOLD CROSSED FIRST TIME. Cumulative tokens_consumed_since_last_rest = ~125k > 100k threshold. thresholds_crossed=['tokens_consumed'] populated. Per cycle A's pre-decision plan: status remains active (no auto-rest), surfacing via this last-verify reason='wellness-threshold-rest-suggested' rather than auto-triggering rest mid-cycle. Founder-decision boundary now LIVE: decide (a) reset tokens per cron fire, (b) raise threshold, (c) auto-trigger rest when crossed-while-active, (d) leave convention and Founder-review per crossing (today's path). (3) NO FOUNDER ACTIVITY in A→B window — most recent commit is 80d354e3 cron-auto-regen from cycle A close (~05:07Z); ~53 min quiet pre-cycle-B-open; consistent with Founder asleep. (4) UNTRACKED TREE UNCHANGED (5 items same as cycle A close); all left alone per isolation discipline. (5) USER-CONTEXT-GATE STALE-COUNTER independently corroborates ~1h gap — delta 13132.8 → 13194.1 min = +61.3 min (would need +300 min for ~5h gap). Two independent measurements agree the gap is ~1h. WELLNESS: status=active throughout (no rest triggered). Counters: tokens accumulate continuously (~125k cumulative, threshold-crossed); hours_active discrete-context (0.5h). Single yellow user-context-gate continues. Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST WITH SELF-CORRECTION. Cycle A's cadence claim was overcalibrated; cycle B's 4th observation corrects it. Token-threshold crossing surfaced proactively rather than after-the-fact. Continuation-discipline 5-question check ran cleanly; Condition A (queued work complete) + G (runbook explicit exit-clean for empty-inbox cycle) cited; stop-decisions NDJSON appended. Eight carry-over Founder action items preserved from cycle A close; #1 (cadence) DOWNGRADED with self-correction note; #8 (token-counter) ESCALATED to threshold-crossed-this-cycle and surfaced via reason field.
founder_action_items: Cron cadence — 20th observation, DOWNGRADED at cycle B close from cycle A's 'ESTABLISHED' back to 'bimodal/multimodal'. Cycle B's ~53 min A→B gap (corroborated independently by user-context stale-counter delta +61.3 min) falsifies cycle A's three-observation 'ESTABLISHED' claim. Cadence has BOTH ~1h gaps (G→K, A→B) and ~5h gaps (E→F, K→L, L→M, M→A). Updated interpretation set: (a) schedule is event-triggered or manual with variable timing; (b) schedule has multiple triggers; (c) the ~5h pattern was coincidental 3-in-a-row alignment; (d) the schedule was actually ~1h all along with occasional missed/failed fires creating ~5h artifacts. Founder-action: inspect cron schedule source directly OR add Step-0 guard. NEW recommendation given bimodality: guard threshold should be ≥60min, `git log --since='6h'` to handle both cadences. Critic metric-integrity self-correction flag: cycle A's 'ESTABLISHED' diagnosis was overcalibrated from a sample of 3; should have demanded 4-6 observations before declaring established. Cycle B corrects.

Possible refinement: post-commit-hook may only write GATE-FAIL on NON-`cron(routine):` subjects (cycle A's auto-regen 80d354e3 should have left PASS in place per this hypothesis); worth confirming next cycle. Founder-action: inspect `.husky/post-commit` source code to confirm rule + subject-pattern mapping. Cycle B stops short of inspecting hook source — Founder-decision boundary for governance change.

Wellness token-counter semantics — THRESHOLD CROSSED THIS CYCLE FOR FIRST TIME. Cumulative tokens_consumed_since_last_rest = ~125k > 100k threshold. thresholds_crossed=['tokens_consumed'] populated. Per cycle A's pre-decision plan: cycle B held status=active (no auto-rest), surfacing via this `reason: 'wellness-threshold-rest-suggested'` field rather than auto-triggering rest. Founder-decision boundary, NOW LIVE not projected: (a) reset tokens per cron fire (matches discrete-context convention for hours_active), (b) raise threshold above heartbeat-cycle granularity, (c) auto-trigger rest when threshold-crossed-while-status-active, (d) leave current convention and Founder-review per crossing. Cycle B chose (d) to honor cycle A's pre-decision; future cycles will continue this path with the cumulative counter growing each cycle until Founder decides.
metric_integrity_corrections_this_cycle: Cycle A close characterized cron cadence as 'ESTABLISHED with 3 direct observations'. Cycle B's 4th observation (~53 min, not ~5h) falsifies the claim. Self-correction: cycle A's diagnosis was overcalibrated from a sample of 3; should have demanded 4-6 more observations before declaring established. Carry-over #1 downgraded from 'ESTABLISHED' to 'bimodal/multimodal' with explicit self-correction note. This is the kind of overconfidence METRIC_INTEGRITY_PROTOCOL is designed to catch; cycle B's adversarial observation provides the necessary correction signal.
